Dupixent Field Dashboard_HelpBox_v6.0.pp.text:
Primary KPIs will reflect your respective territory’s Performance Metrics
Logged-in user’s territory code and name will be displayed here
Navigate to the Sales Data Inquiry Form
**Note: Filters and data on the view will be subset based on your assigned Sales team

Metric Definitions
QTD Volume | Units sold in Quarter Till Date time period at Territory Level
Nat. QTD Vol. | Units sold in Quarter Till Date time period at Nation Level
LTD Volume | Units sold in Launch Till Date time period at Territory Level
Nat. LTD Vol. | Units sold in Launch Till Date time period at Nation Level
QTD Sales Attainment | Ratio of Units sold in QTD time period to its respective QTD goal at Territory Level
Nat. Sales Attn. | Ratio of Units sold in QTD time period to its respective QTD goal at Nation Level
Growth (13) | % Growth calculated at rolling 13 weeks time period
Previous QTR Volume | Units sold in Previous Quarter at Territory Level
Growth (vs Prev QTR) | % Growth calculated at QTD Time Period
Vol Change (vs Prev QTR) | Volume Change calculated at QTD Time Period

Click Here for more filters
Navigate to the Sales Data Inquiry Form
**Note: Filters and data on the view will be subset based on your assigned Sales team

Metric Definitions
C<Time Bucket> TRx | Units sold in current <Time Bucket> time period
P<Time Bucket> TRx | Units sold in previous <Time Bucket> time period
% Change <Time Bucket> TRx | % change in units sold in <Time Bucket> current & previous time period
C<Time Bucket> In-Network Rx | Total number of Patients (with Dupixent Scripts) in current <Time Bucket>
% Change <Time Bucket> In-Network | % change in total number of patients in <Time Bucket> current & previous time period
C13W % Patients PSP Util. | % of In Network Rx’d patients with PSP enrollment in current 13 weeks
P13W % Patients PSP Util. | % of In Network Rx’d patients with PSP enrollment in previous 13 weeks
13 Wks. % Pat. PSP Utilized | Difference in current and previous % of In Network Rx’d patients with PSP enrollments
LTD Conversion Rate | % of In Network Rx'd patients with a dispense from Commercial SP
<Segment> Targets | Total number of targets for the specific <Segment>
QTD <Segment> Interactions | Total number of Interactions for the specific <Segment> in QTD time period
QTD <Segment> Reach | % of Target reach for the specific <Segment> in QTD Time Period
QTD <Segment> Freq. | Ratio of Total number of interactions to the total number of target customers for the specific <Segment>

Click Here for more filters
Select any Customer to view the associated patients in the table below
Navigate to the Sales Data Inquiry Form
**Note: Filters and data on the view will be subset based on your assigned Sales team

Metric Definitions
In-Network Rx’d Patients | Total number of patients (with Dupixent Scripts)
In-Network Dispensed | Total number of patients with a dispense from Commercial SP
HCPs with In Network Rx’d | Total number of HCPs with In Network Rx’d Patient
%Targets with In Network Rx’d | % of Target HCP with In-Network Rx’d patient
%Targets with Dispensed | % of Target HCP with patient dispensed from Commercial SP to Total Targets
% of Rx’d utilizing PSP | % of In Network Rx’d patients with PSP enrollment
Conversion Rate | % of In Network Rx'd patients with a dispense from Commercial SP
Time to Fill | Average of Time to First Fill

Export functionality is disabled on this view
Navigate to the Sales Data Inquiry Form
**Note: Filters and data on the view will be subset based on your assigned Geography

Select a measure for the charts on the right
Click Here for more filters
Navigate to the Sales Data Inquiry Form
**Note: Filters and data on the view will be subset based on your assigned Sales team

Metric Definitions
TRx | Sum of Normalized TRx (for Xponent) and Commonized units (for DDD)
QTD Interactions | Total number of interactions in current quarter
In-Network Rx’d | Total number of Patients (with Dupixent Scripts)
In-Network First Dispense | Total number of patients with a first time dispense of Dupixent
Conversion Rate | % of In Network Rx'd patients with a dispense from Commercial SP
Time to Fill | Average of Time to First Fill

Select a measure for the table on the right
Navigate to the Sales Data Inquiry Form
**Note: Filters and data on the view will be subset based on your assigned Sales team

Metric Definitions
TRx | Sum of Normalized TRx (for Xponent) and Commonized units (for DDD)
QTD Interactions | Total number of interactions in current quarter
In-Network Rx’d | Total number of Patients (with Dupixent Scripts)
In-Network First Dispense | Total number of patients with a first time dispense of Dupixent
Conversion Rate | % of In Network Rx'd patients with a dispense from Commercial SP
Time to Fill | Average of Time to First Fill

Navigate to the Sales Data Inquiry Form
**Note: Filters and data on the view will be subset based on your assigned Sales team

Metric Definitions
Curr. <Wk.> TRx | Count of total TRx in current <week(s)>
Prev. <Wk.> TRx | Count of total TRx in previous <week(s)>
TRx Change <Wk.> | Change in TRx over a period of certain <week(s)>

Select Time Period to reflect the sales metric on desired time bucket
Select metric to filter below information either on Market Volume/Share
To view sales at an individual channel level, please select desired channel description
Channel column has been grouped to DDD & Pharmacy as per below rule: * DDD – DDD & DDD (867) * Pharmacy – Retail, SPP Mail Order & Puerto Rico
To view DDD No. & Address columns, select DDD from Channel column and additional columns will pop-up between the ‘Customer Name’ and ‘Territory Name’
Navigate to the Sales Data Inquiry Form
**Note: Filters and data on the view will be subset based on your assigned Sales team

Metric Definitions
<Product> <Time Period> – Wk TRx | <Time Period> Week TRx for the particular <Product>
Channel Descriptions
Channel | Channel Description | Definition | Frequency
DDD | DDD | Non-Retail sales captured through DDD channel | Weekly
DDD | DDD (867) | Additional Non-Retail sales captured through 867 channel | Monthly
Pharmacy | MAIL ORDER | Drugs purchased through mail orders | Weekly
Pharmacy | PUERTO RICO | Drugs purchased in Puerto Rico Island | Weekly
Pharmacy | RETAIL | Retail Sales Data | Weekly
Pharmacy | SPP | Drugs purchased through Specialty Pharmacies | Weekly

Navigate to the Sales Data Inquiry Form
**Note: Filters and data on the view will be subset based on your assigned Sales team

Metric Definitions
Target HCPs | Target Customers within geography (as per Call Plan published by Field Deployment team)
In-Network Rx’d by Targets | Total number of Patients (with Dupixent Scripts) written by Target HCPs
Target HCPs with In-Network Rx’d by Targets | Total number of target HCPs with In-Network Rx’d patients by Targets
In-Network Dispensed by Targets | Total number of Patients dispensed by Target HCPs
Target HCPs with In-Network Dispensed | Total number of target HCPs with a patient dispensed from Commercial SP
QTD Interactions with Targets | Total Interactions in current quarter done for the target customers
Targets with Interaction | Number of target customers with interactions
Conversion Rate | % of In Network Rx'd patients with a dispense from Commercial SP
Time to Fill | Average of Time to First Fill
QTD Interactions | Total number of interactions in current quarter

AD Penetration Index
(Refer to next slide for Metric definition)
Only valid for DERM Sales Team
Click Here for more filters
All the highlighted customers have no interactions in last 60 days
Navigate to the Sales Data Inquiry Form
**Note: Filters and data on the view will be subset based on your assigned Sales team

Metric Definitions
In-Network Rx’d Patients | Total number of Patients (with Dupixent Scripts)
QTD Interactions | Total number of interactions in current quarter
Dupi TRx | Total number of Dupixent TRx
In-Network Dispensed | Total number of Patients with a dispense from Commercial SP
Conversion Rate | % of In Network Rx'd patients with a dispense from Commercial SP
Time to Fill | Average of Time to First Fill
% of Rx’d Patients utilizing PSP | % of In Network Rx'd patients with PSP enrollment
AD Penetration Index (R12M) | Potential (measured in rolling 12 month time period) of an HCP to prescribe Dupixent to its uncontrolled moderate to severe AD patient population
Dupi AD NBRx from Claims Data (R12M) | New Dupixent patients (AD NBRx) are those who have either
Started on Dupixent in the last 12 months
Restarted on Dupixent after a greater than two year drug holiday
Mod to Sev Uncontrolled AD Patients from Claims Data (R12M) | Total of New Dupixent Patients (defined above) and Bio Naïve AD patients 
Bio Naïve AD patients are those patients who haven’t been treated with any Biologic drug and have a non-biologic claim as per the uncontrolled moderate and severe definition, in 12 month treatment history

Select a Customer to view details
AD Penetration Index
(Refer to next slide for Metric definition)
Only valid for DERM Sales Team
Click Here for more filters
Navigate to the Sales Data Inquiry Form
**Note: Filters and data on the view will be subset based on your assigned Sales team

Metric Definitions
Days since Last Interaction | Number of days since last interaction with an HCP
First Dupi TRx | Date on which the first Dupixent Script was written by an HCP
In-Network Rx’d Patients | Total number of Patients (with Dupixent Scripts)
Conversion Rate | % of In Network Rx'd patients with a dispense from Commercial SP
Time to Fill | Average of Time to First Fill
QTD Interactions | Total number of interactions in current quarter
Dupi TRx | Total number Dupixent Scripts written by an HCP
AD Penetration Index | Potential (measured in rolling 12 month time period) of a HCP to prescribe Dupixent to its uncontrolled moderate to severe AD patient population
Dupi AD NBRx from Claims Data (R12M) | New Dupixent patients (AD NBRx) are those who have either
Started on Dupixent in the last 12 months
Restarted on Dupixent after a greater than two year drug holiday
Mod to Sev Uncontrolled AD Patients from Claims Data (R12M) | Total of New Dupixent Patients (defined above) and Bio Naïve AD patients 
Bio Naïve AD patients are those patients who haven’t been treated with any Biologic drug and have a non-biologic claim as per the uncontrolled moderate and severe definition, in 12 month treatment history

Interaction Data is reported till Interaction Data Through : Date present at the bottom of the view
Select the desired dimension from View by to view the below chart
Select any Geography to drill down
Navigate to the Sales Data Inquiry Form
All the highlighted customers have no interactions in last 60 days
Cut-Off Date for Interaction Data
**Note: Filters and data on the view will be subset based on your assigned Sales team

Metric Definitions
QTD Interactions | Total number of interactions in current quarter
HCPs with Interaction | Total number of unique HCPs having at least a single interaction
% Target with Interaction | % of target HCPs with at least a single interaction
Frequency | Total number of interactions / target customers
Days Since Last Interaction | Number of days since last interaction with an HCP
YTD SGZ Interactions | Total number of interactions made by any Sanofi-Genzyme rep in the current year till date
Total SGZ Interactions | Total number of interaction made by any Sanofi-Genzyme rep in rolling 24 months

Average Penetration Index
(Refer to next slide for Metric definition)
Only valid for DERM Sales Team
Click Here for more filters
Navigate to the Sales Data Inquiry Form
**Note: Filters and data on the view will be subset based on your assigned Sales team

Metric Definitions
Goal Attainment | % ratio of QTD Sales and QTD Goals
In-Network Rx’d Patients | Total number of Patients (with Dupixent Scripts)
Interactions | Total number of interactions
Dupixent TRx | Total number of Dupixent Scripts
QTD AD TRx | Quantity of units sold in QTD time period
Remaining AD Goal | Difference between QTD Goals & QTD Sales
In-Network Dispensed | Total number of Patients with a dispense from Commercial SP
Conversion Rate | % of In Network Rx'd patients with a dispense from Commercial SP
% of Rx’d Patients utilizing PSP | % of In Network Rx'd patients with PSP enrollment
HCPs with In-Network Rx’d | Total number of HCPs with In Network Rx'd patient
HCPs with In-Network Dispensed | Total number of HCPs with a patient dispensed from Commercial SP
Average Penetration Index (Underlying HCPs) (R12M) | Average of Penetration Index of underlying HCPs in a respective geography

Navigate to the Sales Data Inquiry Form
**Note: Filters and data on the view will be subset based on your assigned Sales team

Metric Definitions
# Samples | Total number of samples dropped to the customers

Metric Definitions
<Product> NBRx Vol | Total New to Brand Rx for the particular <Product>
Dupixent Asthma NTS Vol | Total New Therapy Starts for Dupixent Asthma

Use the selector to view the bar chart at the desired grain
Select a Event to view the attendee details in below table
Navigate to the Sales Data Inquiry Form
**Note: Filters and data on the view will be subset based on your assigned Sales team

Metric Definitions
#Closed/Completed | Total number of Reconciled and Completed Events
#Pending/Confirmed | Total number of Pending Event Confirmation and Approved
#Total Attended | Total number of HCPs attended the speaker program events
Invited | Total number of HCPs invited for the speaker program events
# Target Attended | Total number of target HCPs attended the speaker program events
Committed Budget | Total budget that has been used till date (at Territory Level)
Remaining Budget | Total budget that is remaining till date (at Territory Level)
Walk-Ins | Total number of Walk-Ins to the events
Attended Non-HCP | Number of Non-HCP attendees

Use the selector to view the below chart at different stages of the event
Navigate to the Sales Data Inquiry Form
Use the selector to view different types of Flex events in the below chart
**Note: Filters and data on the view will be subset based on your assigned Sales team

Metric Definitions
Closed/Completed | Total number of Reconciled and Completed Events
Pending/Confirmed | Total number of Pending Event Confirmation and Approved
Average Cost Per Event | Average of Total Cost for all events to the total number of Events
Average Cost Per Attendee | Average of Total cost for all events to the total tumber of attendees including non - HCPs
Committed Budget | Total budget that has been used till date (at Territory Level)
Remaining Budget | Total budget that is remaining till date (at Territory Level)
Target % | % of attended Target HCPs
Non Target % | % of attended Non Target HCPs

Dupixent Leadership Dashboard_HelpBox_v6.text: Navigate to the Sales Data Inquiry Form

Metric Definitions
AD/Asthma QTD Volume | Units sold in Quarter Till Date time period at Geography Level
AD/Asthma LTD Volume | Units sold in Launch Till Date time period at Territory Level
AD/Asthma QTD National NBRx | NBRx Volume in Quarter Till Date time period
QTD AD/Asthma IC Volume | IC aligned Units sold in Quarter Till Date time period at Geography Level
AD/Asthma IC Sales Attainment | Ratio of IC aligned Units sold in QTD time period to its respective QTD goal at Territory Level
IC DDD Factor | IC DDD factor, derived from SNSOB data
Previous QTR AD/Asthma IC Volume | IC aligned Units sold in Previous Quarter at Territory Level
AD/Asthma IC Growth (vs Prev QTR) | % Growth calculated at QTD Time Period
Ad/Asthma IC Vol Change (vs Prev QTR) | Volume Change calculated at QTD Time Period

C<Time Bucket> TRx | Units sold in current <Time Bucket> time period

C<Time Bucket> In-Network Rx | Total number of Patients (with Dupixent Scripts) in current <Time Bucket>
% Change <Time Bucket> In-Network | % change in total number of patients in <Time Bucket> current & previous time period
C13W % Patients PSP Util. | % of In Network Rx’d patients with PSP enrollment in current 13 weeks
P13W % Patients PSP Util. | % of In Network Rx’d patients with PSP enrollment in previous 13 weeks
13 Wks. % Pat. PSP Utilized | Difference in current and previous % of In Network Rx’d patients with PSP enrollments
LTD Conversion Rate | % of In Network Rx'd patients with a dispense from Commercial SP
<Segment> Targets | Total number of targets for the specific <Segment>
QTD <Segment> Interactions | Total number of Interactions for the specific <Segment> in QTD time period
QTD <Segment> Reach | % of Target reach for the specific <Segment> in QTD Time Period
QTD <Segment> Freq. | Ratio of Total number of interactions to the total number of target customers for the specific <Segment>

Age Bucket Definitions
From some SPs (E.g., Accredo, Briovarx, etc.), we receive dummy YOB values which corresponds to certain age buckets
Age Bucket | Lower Age Limit | Upper Age Limit
0001 | 0 | 17
0002 | 18 | 39
0003 | 40 | 59
0004 | 60 | ∞
9900 | 0 | 4
9905 | 5 | 9
9910 | 10 | 14
9915 | 15 | 19
9920 | 20 | 24
9925 | 25 | 29
9930 | 30 | 34
Age Bucket | Lower Age Limit | Upper Age Limit
9935 | 35 | 39
9940 | 40 | 44
9945 | 45 | 49
9950 | 50 | 54
9955 | 55 | 59
9960 | 60 | 64
9965 | 65 | 69
9970 | 70 | 74
9975 | 75 | 79
9980 | 80 | 84
9985 | 85 | ∞

Score | Average of the FCR scores

KPIs above are calculated using last 7 days data from the Last Sync through : date
For eg. If Last Sync through date is 4/29/2019 then the KPIs are calculated using data from 4/23/2019 till 4/29/2019
Navigate to the Sales Data Inquiry Form

Metric Definitions
Active Field Personnel | Total number of active employees in the system
Login Within 7 days | Total number of employees who have logged in recent 7 days
% Login within 7 days | % of employees who logged in recent 7 days to the total number of employees
Sync Within 7 days | Total number of employees who have synced data in recent 7 days
% Sync Within 7 days | % of employees who synced data in recent 7 days to the total number of employees
Interactions | Total number of interactions
In-Network Rx’d Patients | Total number of Patients (with Dupixent Scripts)
In-Network Dispensed Patients | Total number of Patients with a dispense from Commercial SP
Conversion Rate | % of In Network Rx'd patients with a dispense from Commercial SP

Meeting with Jacob - Diamond Journey and.section_hint: Diamond evolved through deliberate trade-offs made to meet urgent business priorities while progressively building toward enterprise-ready foundation.
Meeting with Jacob - Diamond Journey and.text:
Diamond evolved through deliberate trade-offs made to meet urgent business priorities while progressively building toward enterprise-ready foundation.
From rapid launch enablement to commercial data foundation
Rapid platform enablement to support critical brand launch when legacy systems could not scale
Diamond expanded into a multi-domain integration layer supporting additional therapeutic areas
Platform transitioned from single-brand solution to enterprise onboarding engine
Diamond becomes the commercial data foundation across brands and products
2022–23
2023–24
2024–25
2025–26
Legacy CIM/SGDW unable to process new brand data volumes
Growing downstream dependencies required operational stability
Multi-brand onboarding and launch readiness became primary priority
Enterprise integration mandate across GenMeds, IC, and FD
Immediate commercial timelines required delivery within ~18 months
Business adoption increased faster than original scope
Standard onboarding pathways introduced to accelerate scale
Strategic decision to sunset legacy CIM processing
Priority placed on launch readiness over enterprise redesign
Organizational and leadership transitions reduced appetite for large re-architecture
Incremental evolution favored over disruptive redesign to protect operations
Platform maturity enables enterprise standardization phase

Meeting with Jacob - Diamond Journey and.section_hint: Enterprise Realities That Shaped Platform Evolution
Meeting with Jacob - Diamond Journey and.text:
Enterprise Realities That Shaped Platform Evolution
Minimize Impact Across Ecosystem
Business continuity across a complex downstream ecosystem was prioritized over structural redesign.
Launch Timelines & Legacy Sunset Pressure
Aggressive launch timelines and committed legacy decommissioning dates constrained opportunities for redesign
TA Evolution and regression considerations
Therapeutic-area needs evolved faster than enterprise alignment mechanisms.
Governance around Architecture Guidelines
Delivery governance emphasized outcomes and speed over continuous architectural recalibration.
Initial Architecture Became Execution Baseline
Outcome Delivery Took Priority Over Structural Optimization
Change Management Cost Influenced Technical Decisions
Limited Central Enterprise Data Architecture Function

Meeting with Jacob - Diamond Journey and.section_hint: Diamond evolved through deliberate trade-offs made to meet urgent business priorities
Meeting with Jacob - Diamond Journey and.text:
Diamond evolved through deliberate trade-offs made to meet urgent business priorities
2022–23: Urgent Build
2023–24: Stabilize & Expand
2024–25: Scale & Enable
2026: Modernized Enterprise
Business Trigger
Legacy system (CIM/SGDW) unable to process Dupixent data (constant issues and failures)
Requirement for diamond platform to onboard Dupixent processes and share process outbounds to CIM/SGDW in legacy format
No formal discovery phase to identify cross brand requirements
Business Context
Integration of different data domains like sales, patient, CRM etc. for 3 more TAs - RBD, Oncology, Kevzara
Increasing downstream dependencies led to lower appetite for change management and overall Retrofitment
Leadership changes (e.g., departure of Susan) altered decision authority
Business Context
Multi-brand onboarding: Dupixent, Rare Disease, Tzield, MS Tolebrutinib on the existing platform
Launch readiness requirements
Business Context
Integration of GenMeds, IC, FD
Mandate to sunset CIM/SGDW
Architectural Trade-Off
Diamond positioned as landing & transformation layer for all the onboarding brands
Maintained downstream data formats to align with data management requirements
Data contracts not enforced enterprise-wide
Key Inflection
Operational continuity prioritized over data rationalization
Decommissioning of legacy system post go live of each brand
Strategic Outcome
Diamond becomes commercial data warehouse for all Brands/Products
Complete retirement of Legacy CIM Processing (Q3’26)
Architectural Decision
Designed for rapid onboarding to cater Dupixent specific business needs
Narrow scope aligned to a single brand requirement not for entire Commercial portfolio
Key Inflection
Architecture shaped for a specific product, not enterprise modeling (brand agnostic)
Architectural Pattern
Standardized onboarding pathway via Diamond
Continued need of retrofitting due to evolving brand needs
Re-architecture deferred due to change management overhead
Key Inflection
Transition from single-brand solution to enterprise scaling engine including all brands
Decommissioning of legacy system post go live of each brand

Meeting with Jacob - Diamond Journey and.section_hint: Diamond evolved through deliberate trade-offs made to meet urgent business priorities while progressively building toward enterprise-ready foundation.
Meeting with Jacob - Diamond Journey and.text:
Diamond evolved through deliberate trade-offs made to meet urgent business priorities while progressively building toward enterprise-ready foundation.
From rapid launch enablement to commercial data foundation
Rapid platform enablement to support critical brand launch when legacy systems could not scale
Diamond expanded into a multi-domain integration layer supporting additional therapeutic areas
Platform transitioned from single-brand solution to enterprise onboarding engine
Diamond becomes the commercial data foundation across brands and products
2022–23
2025–26
2023–24
2024–25
Business Imperatives
Legacy CIM/SGDW unable to process new brand data volumes
Immediate commercial timelines required delivery within ~18 months
Priority placed on launch readiness over enterprise redesign
Multi-brand onboarding and launch readiness became primary priority
Standard onboarding pathways introduced to accelerate scale
Incremental evolution favored over disruptive redesign to protect operations
Enterprise integration mandate across GenMeds, IC, and FD
Strategic decision to sunset legacy CIM processing
Platform maturity enables enterprise standardization phase
Growing downstream dependencies required operational stability
Business adoption increased faster than original scope
Organizational and leadership transitions reduced appetite for large re-architecture

Issues and Reasons
Theme | Description | Impact
Speed vs. Sustainability | The platform was developed under tight timelines (≤ 1.5 years) with no formal discovery phase.
The urgent onboarding of Dupixent took priority over enterprise re-architecture.
Design decisions were optimized for rapid deployment, not long-term scalability or standardization. | The system met immediate business needs but accumulated architectural constraints that limit flexibility and modernization.
Brand Urgency vs. Enterprise Standardization | The architecture was shaped primarily by the needs of Dupixent, rather than a holistic enterprise model.
Dupixent could not be processed in the legacy system, requiring a fast-track solution.
Coding and logic were often tailored by Therapeutic Areas (TAs)
Enterprise-wide data modeling (sum of all needs approach) was not completed. | The platform became brand-driven rather than standardized, creating fragmentation across Therapeutic Areas (TAs).
Operational Continuity vs. Data Rationalization | Maintaining business continuity outweighed structural data cleanup.
Downstream systems required data in the same format.
Full data rationalization would have broken existing integrations.
Data contracts were not enforced across the board.
The sunsetting of CIM/SGDW/DSS accelerated migration without redesign time. | Diamond operated primarily as a landing and transformation layer, rather than a fully rationalized enterprise warehouse.
Directive Decisions vs. Architectural Vision | Governance dynamics influenced architectural outcomes.
Some decisions were directive rather than architecturally driven.
Proposed improvements were highlighted but not fully executed.
Leadership changes (e.g., departure of Susan) altered decision authority.
High change management overhead discouraged re-architecture. | Strategic architectural evolution slowed, reinforcing tactical delivery choices.

Diamond Journey
Q1’24 Rare Blood Disorder Integration and Downstream enablement
Q3’24 US CRM, Oncology, Kevzara Integration & Downstream enablement
Q4’24 One Product & Dupixent Integration with Downstream enablement
Q1’26 GenMeds Integration and Downstream Enablement
Q2’26 IC and FD Downstream Enablement
Q3’26 Retiring legacy CIM Processing completely
Q2’22 Diamond Kick Off
Q3’22 Diamond Architecture Finalization
Q1’23 Dupixent and Kevzara SP and Patient Data
Q2’23 Dupixent Retail, Non-Retail and other data sources
Q1’25 RD Integration & Downstream Enablement
Q2’25 Tzield Integration & Downstream Enablement
Q3’25 MS Tolebrutinib Product Launch readiness, Integration & Downstream enablement

Issues and Reasons
S. No. | Issue | Description
1 | Source Data Rationalization | TA wise Roadmap to enable diamond, leading to lack of clarity of all expected future data sources
Priority to enable Dupixent Foundation in diamond, focusing on Dupixent design rather than designing the Commercial Warehouse
Requirements to use same source data as legacy reducing the opportunity to rationalize feeds from different products
Diamond was a base system and source for multiple parallel systems like Turing, Sapphire. Thus, diamond layouts were in sync with the requirements for parallel systems as well
2 | Downstream Specific redundant datasets | Due to TA-by-TA Integration, Any changes to existing production deliverables led to additional change management overhead
Timeline Crunch, due to pre aligned legacy decommissioning timelines focus was on timely delivery instead of robust and scalable design
3 | Data Architecture | Data Layers initial aligned with the Sanofi DE Team, was reviewed and aligned but due to committed timelines the design was never looked back or retrofitted to improve

Diamond Journey

2023
2024
2025
2026

PSS A&R
~185

Team planning: ask from Sanofi – the capacity we need on PM and gov | review | Operating Model

Project Assumptions (1/2)
Requirements & Inputs Assumptions
PSS A&R and ICI team to provide complete, accurate, and timely business requirements for non-Dupixent TAs and downstream consumers using the ZS Business and Data requirements template
ZS will receive all necessary contextual information on use cases, KBQs, KPIs, and downstream reporting needs
Any changes or additions to requirements may impact timelines and effort post the requirements phase closure
Requirements from PSS A&R and ICI will be signed off before build begins
Dependencies on upstream systems (e.g., source data owners) will be managed by Sanofi teams
Data Availability & Quality Assumptions
Required source data for finalized CADs and RRDs will be available and accessible to Design and Development team
Data issues (missing fields, unexpected values, quality problems) will be resolved by Digital/Source System owners
All the relevant infrastructure, systems/database accesses, schema-level accesses will be provided by Sanofi Digital to support the project
Data Validations will depend on representative sample outputs being provided by Digital
Design & Build Assumptions
ZS Team will be onboarded on to the existing ICI Data Model and PADs and design of up to 2 CADs and 10 RRDs are assumed as part of scope
ZS and ICI to ensure that the proposed Data Products will be scalable across TAs and markets
Design inputs (BRD, KPI mapping, metadata, etc.) will be treated as source of truth during implementation
Any architectural constraints or sign-offs (OneMesh, iCare Insights ecosystem) will be clarified with Digital team
Platform (iCare Insights, Snowflake, PBI ecosystem, etc.) is stable, accessible, and managed by Digital including DevOps
The metadata creation for the CADs and RRDs will include – table and attribute names and detailed descriptions, along with ontologies/tags in English for 2 CADs and 10 RRDs

Project Assumptions (2/2)
Downstream Retrofitment Assumptions
PBI dashboard retrofitment to be performed by Sanofi Hub team, timeline assumed is 8-9 weeks for the same
ZS provides guidance, SOPs, and oversight and other guidance on hands-on retrofitment for initial 6 weeks
Validation & Testing Assumptions
The estimates are based on the assumption that ZS and ICI will be co-responsible for designing the CADs, STTMs, development of code, unit testing, and end to end testing of scoped CADs/RRDs
All defects must be logged, triaged and resolved jointly
UAT coordination, environment readiness, and user access are owned by Sanofi Digital
UAT sign-off timelines depend on stakeholder availability from PSS A&R
Governance & Collaboration Assumptions
Cross-team governance meetings will be scheduled consistently (weekly/bi-weekly sessions + milestone reviews).
Decision logs and risk logs maintained by ZS will be accepted as formal artifacts.
Timeline & Estimation Assumptions
Major requirement changes, new KPIs, new data sources, or new TAs may require timeline and cost adjustments.
Parallel analytics workstreams will continue independently without impacting this program
